title: The Query That Should Not Exist
description:
Tunde: 'The .env they pulled had the database credentials in plaintext. But they were inside the login form before they even used those. We have the MySQL general query log from that night. One query in there should not be possible — it does not match any legitimate application flow. Find it.'

Amaka: 'When you find the anomalous query, read what it returned. The server logged the first matched row. That row contains a field that is not plain text. Decode it — Cipher Tools in another browser tab handles that format — and follow where it points.'

welcome: MySQL query log extract mounted read-only at /sqllog/
Type `help` for commands, `ls -la` to list including hidden files.

files./sqllog/README.md:
MySQL general query log extract, 2024-01-14 02:00–04:00 UTC.
Produced by: mysqld --general-log --general-log-file=/var/log/mysql/general.log

This log records every query the application sent to the database.
Most entries are routine. One is not.
files./sqllog/mysql-general.log:
